level_signals: 4+ years of product management experience focused on backend, platform, or API products
2+ years leading multiple third-party integrations with enterprise customers or vendors
Comp range $165K–$174K CAD, consistent with Senior IC level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years of product management experience focused on backend, platform, or API products.
2+ years leading multiple third-party integrations with enterprise customers or vendors from technical discovery to go-live, against tight timelines.
Proven end-to-end delivery of complex, multi-stakeholder integration programs (built roadmaps, wrote specs, drove cross-functional execution, and launched to production).
Experience communicating technical concepts to C-suite stakeholders and going deep with engineering; demonstrated success running partner scope reviews and negotiating SOWs/SLAs.
Bachelor's degree. Proficiency with APIs and data contracts. Comfortable using AI tools to accelerate PM workflows; still capable of writing PRDs without them.

Bachelor's degree. Proficiency with APIs and data contracts. Comfortable using AI tools to accelerate PM workflows; still capable of writing PRDs without them.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Retail technology domain expertise across brick-and-mortar systems such as POS, inventory, loyalty, coupons, or store operations platforms.
Experience building or integrating AI/ML or computer vision capabilities, including defining data needs for model training, inference, and feedback loops.
Background in integration reliability and data quality (monitoring, observability, alerting, error budgets, and SLAs).
Experience handing off your work to other internal teams to scale (e.g. playbook writing)
Familiarity with privacy, security, and compliance for retail data (e.g., PCI considerations, PII handling, data retention policies).

explicitly_excluded: Hiring only in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, and Nova Scotia (Canadian provinces); US-based candidates excluded for this posting

scope.team_size_estimate: 11 direct teammates (majority backend engineers); broader Connected Stores org of a few hundred people
scope.surface: In-Store Platform — APIs, data pipelines, and foundational integrations powering Caper carts, Foodstorm catering, Carrot Tags, and AI-driven store intelligence
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS / platform — integrations sold/licensed to grocery retailers
cross_functional_partners: External retail technology providers (POS, loyalty, inventory, coupon system vendors)
core_responsibilities: Own the integration roadmap for Connected Stores: strategy, scope, and prioritization based on business impact, data value, and technical feasibility
Drive end-to-end execution: align Engineering, Partnerships, Solution Architecture, and 3rd party teams from discovery through launch and scale
Lead external partner alignment with retailers and software vendors; define data contracts and ensure technical fit
Ship first-of-a-kind integrations including those powering new AI products; document patterns and transition repeatable work to Solution Architects
Establish SOWs and SLAs; draft requirements, timelines, and deliverables; negotiate partner responsibilities
Set and measure KPIs such as time-to-integrate, data coverage and quality, reliability, and product adoption
Resolve conflicts and remove blockers across internal product needs and external constraints

compensation.equity_form: new hire equity grant and annual refresh grants

green_flags: Clear team size stated (11 direct teammates, broader org of a few hundred)
Well-defined product surface and ownership scope
Equity (new hire + annual refresh) explicitly mentioned
Flex First fully remote policy clearly stated
Specific, concrete responsibilities — not vague boilerplate
AI focus is substantive and tied to real technical constraints (data foundations for models)
red_flags: Hiring restricted to four Canadian provinces only (Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Nova Scotia) — excludes all US and most international candidates
Compensation stated in CAD only; no USD range provided, which may deter US-based candidates who might otherwise be eligible
First-of-its-kind integrations work implies significant ambiguity and pioneer risk with limited established playbooks
Hard requirement of 2+ years leading enterprise third-party integrations end-to-end may be difficult to demonstrate outside enterprise SaaS or retail tech backgrounds

work_auth and visa_sponsorship not addressed in JD body; left as 'unclear'
